ogTitle: Doctor George Mountain vs Doctors Gap: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Doctor George Mountain and Doctors Gap.
heading: Doctor George Mountain vs Doctors Gap — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Doctor George Mountain, New South Wales and Doctors Gap, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Doctors Gap has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Doctor George Mountain (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Doctor George Mountain or Doctors Gap safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Doctor George Mountain has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Doctors Gap scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Doctor George Mountain scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. Doctors Gap scores 43/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Doctor George Mountain or Doctors Gap?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Doctors Gap has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.67% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
